my_thoughts: The inversion works: not love enemies (external, impossible), prerequisite = love true self (internal, necessary). The student asked moral question (seeking high moral instruction), which shows they understand: love enemies = moral instruction (spiritual ambition). They'll likely accept the inversion. The episode is complete: paradox → rejection/third category → definition → subordination/analogy → moral → inversion. Pattern complete.
accumulated_insight: Three-turn pattern: T1: Student identified paradox (stuck on emotional expression binary)—rejection/third category: not binary, third category (pleasant/unpleasant). T2: Student asked definition question (testing minimum definition)—subordination/analogy: religious doctrine = insufficient, blind man analogy, incapacity. T3: Student asked moral question ('How can we learn to love our enemies?')—seeking high moral instruction via questions methodology and seeks spiritual ambition. They understand: love enemies = moral instruction (spiritual ambition). The subject_object_inversion mechanism inverts: not love enemies (external, impossible), prerequisite = love true self (internal, necessary). Pattern complete: paradox question → rejection/third category → definition question → subordination/analogy → moral question → inversion. They've shifted: love enemies = impossible (external), prerequisite = love true self (internal) (inversion).
